name: basal_ganglia
description: Basal ganglia — Go/NoGo gating circuit. Implements direct (D1, Go) and indirect (D2, NoGo) pathways. Controls which cortical targets update vs maintain via thalamic disinhibition. Stripes 0-2: PFC working memory. Stripe 3: Broca's Merge permission (putamen channel). Stripe 4: temporal cortex token intake.

nodes.description: Direct pathway D1 MSN for PFC stripe 0. When active: inhibits GPi → disinhibits thalamus → gate opens → WM updates.
D1 MSN for Broca's area (anterior putamen). When active: inhibits GPi_3 → disinhibits VA thalamus → BA44 Merge trigger receives permission signal.
D1 MSN for temporal cortex token intake.
Indirect pathway D2 MSN for PFC stripe 0. When active: excites GPi (via GPe→STN→GPi) → inhibits thalamus → gate stays closed → WM maintains.
D2 MSN for PFC stripe 2 (goal — usually active, keeping goal stable).
D2 MSN for Broca. Active → Merge inhibited (keep building, not ready to Merge yet).
External globus pallidus — tonically active, inhibited by D2 NoGo.
Subthalamic nucleus — excitatory. Receives from GPe (inhibitory). When GPe is suppressed by NoGo, STN fires and excites GPi, reinforcing the gate-closed state.
Internal globus pallidus for PFC stripe 0. Tonically active (inhibits thalamus by default).
GPi for Broca. Controls VA thalamic relay. Tonically inhibits → Merge blocked by default. Go pathway must fire to permit Merge.

Dopamine signal (reward prediction error). Positive RPE strengthens D1 (Go) and weakens D2 (NoGo) for the actions that led to reward. Negative RPE does the opposite. This is how the system LEARNS when to gate.

edges.description: Direct pathway: D1 Go INHIBITS GPi (negative weight). Disinhibits thalamus.
Broca Go → inhibit GPi_3 → disinhibit VA thalamus → Merge permitted.
Indirect pathway step 1: D2 NoGo INHIBITS GPe.
Indirect step 2: GPe INHIBITS STN. When GPe is suppressed by NoGo, STN is disinhibited.
Indirect step 3: STN EXCITES GPi (positive weight). Reinforces gate-closed.
Go and NoGo compete within each stripe via lateral inhibition.
Dopamine excites D1 (Go) MSNs — positive RPE strengthens Go.
Dopamine inhibits D2 (NoGo) MSNs — positive RPE weakens NoGo.

properties.default_state: GPi tonically active → thalamus inhibited → gates closed. Go pathway must actively fire to open a gate. This means WM holds by DEFAULT — you need a signal to update, not a signal to hold.
properties.dopamine_role: Reward prediction error. Positive: strengthens the gating decisions that led to reward. Negative: weakens them. Over time, the system learns WHEN to open each gate.
properties.broca_channel: Stripe 3 is the anterior putamen channel for Broca's Merge. BA44 projects here. GPi_3 controls VA thalamic relay. The system must LEARN when to permit Merge — initially random, shaped by reward.
properties.temporal_channel: Stripe 4 controls the temporal cortex relay for token intake.
